exports.content: Applying external compression to commensurate twisted bilayer graphene with twist angles from about 9.4 degrees to about 27.8 degrees can produce narrow flat electronic bands and near-Fermi van Hove singularities in large-angle regimes that are single-layer-graphene-like at ambient pressure; pressure is therefore a feasible control parameter for flat-band engineering in easier-to-fabricate larger-angle devices and motivates, but does not demonstrate, possible correlated phases [@Ge2021].
